(off) What's your name?

As the singer begins to proselytize, the camera combs the audience of STREET KIDS and comes to focus on GREG, a skinny, 16-year-old speed freak in a "Metallica" t-shirt who fits right in with this crowd. Beside him is LITTLE J, a girlish-looking 14-year-old who compensates with a pint-sized tough-guy routine. Sucking petulantly on a cigarette, Little J acts like he's been dragged to the opera.

With that, the band launches into a Christian anthem. Some of the crowd--especially those near the front--are really into it. And as the guitarist steps forward and launches into a solo, several in the audience--Greg included--join in on air guitar.
ANGLE ON STAGE

Just as the guitarist is reaching the apogee of his solo, WHUMP!--a large paper bag drops onto stage and bursts open, showering the band in flour. The music grinds to a halt am~d MUCH LAUGHTER. The singer, looking like Caspar the Friendly Ghost, barks indignantly into his microphone.

AT THE BACK OF THE HALL

The LIGHTING MAN swings the spotlight around, scanning the hall and coming to rest on a small balcony above the stage, where stands a young man in a faded blue bandanna-- King. As the beam of light pins him down, the crowd slowly falls silent. CLOSE ON GREG AND LITTLE J as they react to the appearance of King. surprised. They seem

The security dudes start up the stairs, closing in on him. King looks around for an escape route. There isn't one. Here come the security apes. King climbs onto the ledge and grits his teeth. The crowd below cheers him on.
(to himself) Jesus, don't fail me now.

With that, he executes a spectacular swan dive into the crowd, and lands in a sea of upheld hands. He's mightily impressed. He tries to escape through the throng, but he's so dazed by his free fall that he's easy prey for the security goons, who collar him and drag him off amid much confusion.

meat-grinders. The guards turn to see Little J pointing a zip gun at them. King sighs.

saving your skin.

This morning.

and now Manny goes for the whip and makes his bid on the outside--

A kid named CRASHER is running as fast as he can down the sidewalk; his long (but thinning) hair flutters over his shoulders. He looks over to see Manny's wheelchair come roll}ng up to.him.

Little J pockets a bottle of India ink.

At the "finish line," Manny raises his hands in victory then clamps them on his wheels and brakes to a stop. Crasher, panting heavily, comes stumbling up after him.
I romped, sucker.
Crasher, short of breath, bends over and holds his knees. How am I supposed to beat a guy who thinks he's a horse. Manny WHINNIES, then reaches in his pocket-MANNY
--takes out a joint and strikes it up. it; he exhales and says, easily: He inhales, holds

still doubled over, still trying to catch his breath.
(off) I don't believe it.

INT. TOMY'S BURGERS - NIGHT

WIDER Little J winces as King again pokes the needle into his ann and removes it, leaving a dot of ink and blood. They sit in a booth, surrounded by Greg, Crasher and Manny; King frowns and pushes the hamburger wrappers to one side of the table, giving himself more space to work. He again pushes the needle under Little J's skin. KING

Little J nods; he looks a little green. Rather than watching his own ann, he looks at King's, which is covered with several homemade and professional tattoos, including one on his bicep of an evil-looking cobra with "KING" written above it.
You gonna crash with us at the hole tonight, King?
King keeps his gaze trained on Little J's ann.
I was hoping to see Devon. seen her?
No, man, I haven't.
(pointedly) What do you mean?

King keeps his eyes on his "canvas."

A long, uneasy silence. Greg looks to Crasher for help; Crasher looks to Manny. Manny stares awkwardly at the floor, then at King. King finally looks up.
(softly) Listen, King .... The last we saw your filly was about two weeks ago. Looks like she's in Tommy Ray's stable now.
You saw Devon with Tommy Ray?
Yeah.
Crasher lowers his eyes.
With that fuckin' pimp?!
(nods) It's true, King.
King stares long and hard at Greg.
Just out of curiosity--were you gonna tell me?
Yeah ..*. Yeah?
(softly) Later.
A long beat as everyone looks a little sheepish. Then King takes crasher's arm and gently pulls him from the booth.
INT. TOMY'S REST ROOM - NIGHT
Crasher leans against the sink with his arms folded. stands looking at him for several beats.

I get locked up for sixty days and everything goes to shit. You know I took a zip gun off of Little J tonight? You know the SHIT he could get in with that? And Greg there looks like he ain't done nothing but snort speed for the last week. And fuck knows where my girlfriend is. Turning tricks, I guess.
Manny's doin' all right .**.
Manny's the only one of you playing with a full deck. And he's nuts!
Long beat. Crasher's only response is to sniff his armpits and wrinkle his nose in distaste. Then he strips off his shirt and begi~s to wash his pits in the sink.
I ASKED you to look out for everyone.

I know, but-(CONTINUED) (hard) But what? You were wrong to ask me to do it. I'm the wrong guy. Nobody listens to me, 'cause nobody respects me. They only respect YOU. But YOU fucked up and got locked up, and THAT screwed everyone up. And that ain't my fault.
The simple truth of Crasher's words register a direct hit on King, who says nothing else. Crasher proceeds to dry his armpits under the automatic hand drier.

Tommy Ray, a muscular sleazeball of a pimp, steps out of a shadowy doorway.
Tommy Ray looks up to see King, flanked by Greg, Crasher and Little J, standing before him.
Well, if it ain't "King," and his merry band of faggots. Fuck you.

Yeah .*.* I could make some cash with you. You got a tight little ass.

You're a piece of shit, Tommy.
out of nowhere, Tommy Ray produces a stiletto, snaps it open* and brandishes it at crasher.

C'mon, King. Yeah.

C'mon.

(calls out) Hey, now, you remember to bring Little J back to me as soon as his pubic hair comes in.
Little J, receding from sight, gives Tommy Ray the finger. Tommy laughs derisively.
Shit *...

King, Crasher, Greg and Little J shuffle along the intersection where Hollywood Boulevard crosses the 101 freeway.
No, I'm even more glad. 'Cause I coulda easily shot that fucker.

About 10 STREET KIDS hang out underneath the overpass. Various discarded mattresses are lined up against the graffiti-covered overpass support. Another supporting wall several feet away hides most of the area from the freeway below. Broken glass litters the ground; discarded blankets and pieces of clothing are scattered here and there. (passes joint, sighs, rubs his eyes) Man, what a drag. My first night out and I'm sleeping alone.

I'll sleep with ya', King.
(laughs easily) Well, I appreciate it, Brenda. you're not my type.
Why not?
*Little J, passing the joint to crasher, giggles. Too fat.
Shut up, fool.
I ain't no fool, bitch.
Yeah you are.
Little J, don't wind her up or she'll run all night.
I know.
You know SHIT, dick-for-brains.

EXT. HILL - NIGHT

Greg sits overlooking the freeway, a needle in his hand and a belt around his bicep. He stares at the blur of headlights for a bit; then, with great precision, he breaks down his rig, wraps it up and stashes it behind a rock.
(off) You're SHOOTING that shit now, huh?

(shrugs) More buzz for the dollar.

P.o.v.

And his eyes come to fall on HEATHER, 17, a slim and delicately pretty girl in jeans and a large wool sweater. She clutches a small duffel bag, and looks nervously around the depot. CLOSE ON HEATHER She is just standing there, plotting her next move, when Tommy Ray steps up with an unlit cigarette between his lips.
Got a light?

(as she lights him) Long ride?
ACROSS THE DEPOT

The pair of hands now wrap the t-shirt around the cans and knot the end of the shirt. WIDE ANGLE Tommy Ray is escorting Heather out of the bus station when, from out of nowhere, a barechested King steps up, and, using his weighted t-shirt like a sap, bongs Tommy Ray over the head. Heather gasps. Tommy Ray topples over like a cartoon character.
Now the others step up. Little J quickly snaps one handcuff on Tommy Ray, then clamps the other to a bolted-down chair.
(dizzily) You little fucks ....
King uncaps a black sharpie, leans over and scrawls the word "PIMP" across Tommy Ray's forehead. Greg and Little J roar with laughter. Then King looks over to see a SECURITY GUARD moving toward them.
Let's go!

--and then joins his pals in bolting from the depot, all of them whooping like Banshees.

(off) Wait!

dicks! This is news to Heather.

C'mon, I'll buy you something to eat.

BUT LET'S GO!

INT.,TOMY'S BURGERS - DAY

Trip out. so am I.
(off) What a fuckin' liar. Phoenix. You're from

Thanks.

King, Heather, Greg, crasher, Little J and Brenda stand in the darkness on the edge of an overpass. Off, the SOUND of an APPROACHING TRAIN.
You're crazy! If we weren't, we'd be crazy!

I'll wait for you.

You can do it!

Then don't worry about it. But if

we're gonna go, we gotta go now! Heather's eyes grow very wide as she looks at him. She takes a deep breath, holds it; she offers him her hand and he takes it. They jump together--landing safely on a boxcar roof, two down from the others. Everyone CHEERS. King stands and strikes a funny, cocky pose, one which reads "total victory!" What do you think?

King laughs, then points up ahead. KIUG ' S P. 0 . V. Little J and Brenda are dancing in the moonlight to the music from the ghetto blaster. ANGLE: HEATHER & KING Heather laughs, watches a moment. Then she looks to King.
What do we do now?
Enjoy the ride.

But I'll never be able to brush my hair again.
King kneels down beside her and then takes off his bandanna.
Here.

He ties it around her forehead; and although it began as a spontaneous gesture, certain latent implications strike both Heather and King as he finishes knotting it. They look into each other's eyes. They say nothing. They don't have to. ANGLE The -spell is broken as Crasher, on a far boxcar, motions to them and cups his hands to his mouth.
Time to jump!
(takes Heather's hand) C'mon.

You guys got a cool way of doing things.

Well, we're sort of like an army right? And we just keep getting bigger. When we get big enough, we're gonna-- take over the city.
Fuck that-- we're gonna take over the whole country!
(with feeling) Yeah.
And then the whole world!
The motherfuckin' universe!
And then what're you gonna do?
Little J grabs Brenda.
All the guys laugh.
Fuck all the chicks!

Get your hand off my tit, you runt.

Your "father." Fuck you.

(off) You do this? (off) A couple years ago, yeah.

Touchy subject? ;.,;-~,. _,,,_

Greg and Little J move along the street, going from car to car. Greg checks the doors on the driver's side, while Little J cases the passenger's side. All the doors are coming up locked. Then Little J glances up a driveway.
Parked in the driveway is a BMW.
